records.title: On-time performance rate
records.summary: Track the percentage of trains arriving within threshold of scheduled time by corridor, service type, and cause of delay.
records.metric_definition: Trains arriving within tolerance divided by total scheduled trains, expressed as a percentage.
records.methodology_note: Use 5-minute threshold for passenger, 30-minute for freight; exclude force majeure events.

records.title: Asset availability rate
records.summary: Measure the proportion of rolling stock and infrastructure assets available for service against planned fleet or network capacity.
records.metric_definition: Available asset-hours divided by total planned asset-hours for the period.

records.title: Safety management system compliance
records.summary: Maintain systematic safety risk assessment, reporting, and mitigation aligned with railway safety regulatory frameworks.

records.title: Interoperability and data standards
records.summary: Adopt open data exchange standards for timetabling, ticketing, and asset data to enable multi-operator and cross-border interoperability.

records.title: Predictive asset maintenance and lifecycle optimization
records.summary: Shift from time-based to condition-based and predictive maintenance for track, rolling stock, and signalling to reduce failures and extend asset life.

records.title: Network capacity and throughput optimization
records.summary: Apply AI-driven scheduling and real-time conflict resolution to maximize train paths per hour on constrained corridors.

records.title: Combine real-time train positioning with infrastructure status for recovery
records.summary: Prioritize use cases that fuse live train GPS/signalling positions with infrastructure condition alerts and crew availability to generate optimal recovery plans during disruption rather than static timetable replanning.

records.title: Link asset condition data to network impact models for renewal prioritization
records.summary: Prefer use cases that connect track, signalling, and rolling stock condition data to network-wide impact models so renewal budgets target the assets whose failure causes the greatest passenger disruption rather than age-based replacement schedules.
